question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The “desktop mail client can’t send; webmail works” symptom matches a documented Exchange/SMTP-AUTH issue where SMTP AUTH was disabled by a tenant security change (Security Defaults), causing legacy Outlook clients’ send attempts to fail with authentication errors. In that prior case the team recreated profiles and performed client checks, then escalated to an Exchange specialist; the root cause was “SMTP AUTH disabled due to Security Default Policy” and the fix was to selectively enable SMTP AUTH for affected users and adjust Conditional Access. This is documented in ticket TI-1548823 (KF22)

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The “scan-to-mail: small scans work, big scans dropped” symptom is similar in part to a documented scan-to-mail incident where the printer VLAN was not allowed by the SMTP relay and external delivery was denied (“Relay denied”). The resolution there was to update the SMTP relay allow list for the printer VLAN and then re-test sending; after the change external scans were delivered again. This is documented in ticket TI-1624175 (KF60)

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The Basel printer that briefly accepts jobs then nothing prints (jobs vanish) matches a documented location-printer incident where the print server could not reach printers after the printers were moved to a new VLAN; the root cause was a missing network/firewall allowance between the print server and the printer VLAN. The fix was to adjust routing/firewall rules so the print server can reach the printer; printing was then restored. This is documented in ticket TI-1622483 (KF60)

In the documented Basel case, the print server could not ping the printer while a client in the printer VLAN could — indicating segmentation/firewall blocking between server and printer

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 02.12.25 09:03
Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

SOURCE 6:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
